items.title: Right sweeps Latin America with new victory in Colombia

items.title: French co-op on a mission to prove organic farming can feed the world
items.description: Many people assume that organic agriculture is expensive and hard to produce on a massive scale. But a group of 10 farmers in France's western Vendée region want to prove that chemical-free farming can feed a whole country. Their co-operative is 100 percent organic and local. Their vegetables are sold to schools, supermarkets and restaurants. The co-op's yields are on par with those of conventional farming. Our Down to Earth team reports.

items.title: Onsemi to buy Synaptics in $7 billion all-stock deal

items.title: Frida Kahlo: London exhibit sheds light on troubled life of Mexican artist
items.description: In this edition, we look at Tate Modern's exhibition dedicated to Mexican painter Frida Kahlo. It displays her famous self-portraits and works by other artists who were influenced by her. Also: French World Cup fans go crazy for Panini sticker albums as newsstands experience shortages. Meanwhile, the Ghanaian music genre of Highlife has been added to UNESCO's list of intangible cultural heritage. Finally, one of France's biggest music festivals, Solidays, kicks off this Friday with French stars Gims, Orelsan, Bigflo & Oli and American EDM group Major Lazer all set to perform.

items.title: French banquets: Fun party or culture war?
items.description: With summer in full swing in France, we take a look at the French festive spirit and how traditional banquets are creating controversy, with reports of racist slurs and Nazi salutes at the events. We also discuss how other gatherings, like free parties and raves, are coming under scrutiny, amidst criticism that they are being instrumentalised politically. Finally, we go into other festivities and how France still excels at "faire la fête", or partying.

items.title: Paris neighbourhoods: The dynamic 10th arrondissement
items.description: In this edition of French Connections, Genie Godula and Florence Villeminot continue their tour of Paris with a stop in the 10th arrondissement of Paris. Located in the heart of the Right Bank, it’s one of the most vibrant parts of the capital. A lot of people enter Paris through the dixième, thanks to the city’s two biggest train stations, the Gare de Nord et Gare de l’Est. They, along with the much-loved Canal Saint Martin, have helped turn the 10th into a multicultural melting pot of restaurants, bars, theatres and other hidden gems that capture the essence of real Parisian life.

items.title: 'Big polluting companies like Total have climate obligations under French law'
items.description: Genie Godula is pleased to welcome Justine Ripoll, Campaign Manager for French NGO "Notre Affaire à Tous", and one of four NGOs who brought the climate case against Total Energy. She argues that climate litigation not as a symbolic confrontation with the fossil fuel industry, but as an evolving mechanism of governance that seeks to clarify and enforce corporate responsibility under climate law. Ripoll shifts the conversation from technological inevitability to political and ethical prioritisation, arguing that the remaining carbon budget should be allocated according to collective social needs rather than commercial interests. She asserts that multinational energy companies can no longer position climate change solely as a matter for governments. Instead, she contends that businesses whose activities substantially contribute to greenhouse gas emissions carry independent legal obligations that courts are increasingly willing to recognise.

items.title: Peter Thiel's broken doorbell – what a week outside his Buenos Aires mansion revealed

items.title: Social infrastructure during heatwave: 'Knock on the door makes the difference between life & death'
items.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Eric Klinenberg, Professor of Sociology at NYU and Author of "Heat Wave: A Social Autopsy of Disaster in Chicago". He challenges one of the dominant assumptions surrounding extreme heat: that technological adaptation alone can protect societies from climate change. Drawing on his landmark study of Chicago's deadly 1995 heatwave, he argues that mortality during extreme heat is shaped as much by the strength of social ties as by meteorological conditions.

items.title: Despite problems at home and abroad, Trump keeps gaining allies in Latin America

items.title: France confirms first ever case of Ebola in doctor returning from DRC
items.description: France on Wednesday announced its first confirmed case of Ebola identified on its territory, a doctor back from the Democratic Republic of the Congo, which is fighting a major outbreak.

items.title: Milei settles Argentina's US$15-million UN debt
items.description: Milei administration pays off Argentina’s outstanding US$15.6-million debt with the United Nations as campaigning for Rafael Grossi's secretary-general candidacy enters key stretch. Leer más

items.title: Trump's 'Alligator Alcatraz' detention center closes after a year of controversy
items.description: Florida's controversial "Alligator Alcatraz" immigration detention centre, a symbol of US President Donald Trump's deportation drive, has closed after operating for less than a year, officials said on Thursday. Florida Governor Ron DeSantis said the Everglades facility had emptied after fulfilling its emergency role despite criticism over detainees' conditions and due process.

items.title: Iran says deal to end West Asia war 'declaration of U.S. defeat'
items.description: The Islamabad understanding was not the result of pressure and coercion, but rather the result of the resistance and authority of the brave Iranian nation, says Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, the head of the Iranian negotiating team

items.title: Stellantis, Nissan eye Marelli assets in rescue talks, Bloomberg reports
Europe is spending billions to rearm, so why the delays?
items.description: European countries have been trying to scale up their military capabilities, but their current defense production and procurement model is not fit for purpose, say experts.

items.title: Live: PM flags strengthening powers of eSafety commissioner for social media ban
items.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says the government will look to strengthen the powers of the eSafety commissioner to enforce an under-16s social media ban. Follow live.

items.title: Argentines in arrears: 1 in every 4 families cannot pay their debts
items.description: In the past year, 2.5 million debtors have fallen behind with payments. Some 3.3 million people currently owe payments dating back more than a year for sums averaging approximately 1.6 million pesos. Private studies show the situation facing families in Argentina. Leer más

items.title: Europe swelters under record-breaking heatwave
items.description: The effects of the extreme weather, with temperatures hotter than parts of east and west Africa, have been made worse by buildings and infrastructure not designed to cope with high temperatures

items.title: HRW says Cameroon not doing enough to combat violence against women
items.description: In a new report, it said violence against women and girls remains widespread despite repeated government promises to do something about it.

items.title: Earthquake is devastating blow to Venezuela at time of uncertainty
items.description: The twin quakes come less than six months after Venezuela's then-leader Nicolás Maduro was seized by US forces.

items.title: UN agency pauses Hormuz ship evacuation initiative after vessel attacked
items.description: Iran warns vessels to use only Tehran-approved routes as IMO halts Hormuz evacuation after ship struck.

items.title: World Cup 2026 knockout stage: Who's in, who's out?
items.description: France 24's Selina Sykes takes a look at the teams already qualified for the knockout stage of the 2026 World Cup and who still has it all to play for as the final round of group games continues.

items.title: India initiates anti-dumping probe against three Chinese products
items.description: Four applications have been filed by separate domestic firms before Commerce Ministry's Directorate General of Trade Remedies to initiate the probe

items.title: ‘Truly terrifying’: Caracas confronts the aftermath of Venezuela earthquake
items.description: Devastated residents recall the moment when they experienced the twin earthquakes that toppled buildings and killed 188.

items.title: BHP-backed I-Pulse wins $250 million US CHIPS award for silicon-carbide semiconductors
2 earthquakes in Venezuela kill at least 164. And, Trump cancels housing bill signing
items.description: Two major earthquakes in Venezuela have killed at least 164 people and left hundreds injured. And, President Trump canceled the signing of a massive bipartisan housing bill yesterday.

items.title: Abalone divers say 41 per cent catch reduction 'a kick in the face'
items.description: The NSW government says a reset is needed to ensure the sustainable future of the abalone fishery along the south coast, but commercial divers say the reduction is a "pretty disgusting state of affairs".

items.title: Argentina approves dollar borrowing amid multilateral loan talks
items.description: Decree establishes a legal framework for future financing transactions, with contracts governed by New York law and subject to the jurisdiction of US courts. Leer más

items.title: Trump airs grievances with NATO chief over alliance's lack of Iran war support
items.description: President Trump met with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte Wednesday, where he shared his frustrations with the lack of support he's received from allies on the Iran war.

items.title: Police release CCTV footage of man missing for three weeks
items.description: Police say the 43-year-old, who has been missing for three weeks, had regularly taken the V/Line train between Colac and Melbourne.

items.title: Lalo Schifrin’s final symphony, Rod Schejtman named Argentine cultural ambassador
items.description: Rod Schejtman, the composer Lalo Schifrin designated as his artistic heir, now carries Argentina’s national brand to the world’s concert halls. Leer más

items.title: Milei leads rightist leaders congratulating Colombian president-elect
items.description: Argentina's President Javier Milei one of the first to congratulate Colombia's hard-right president-elect Abelardo de la Espriella. Leer más

items.title: Japanese sake brewery to make foray into Indian market
items.description: Takahashi Shouten, based in the city of Yame in Fukuoka Prefecture, launched a subsidiary in India in February.

items.title: Fact check: No, Zelenskyy wasn't killed in a Russian airstrike
items.description: Amid a flare-up in violence between Russia and Ukraine, viral posts claim Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has died. DW Fact check takes a closer look.

items.title: WNBA: Alyssa Thomas suspended one game for fist into Cailtin Clark’s throat
items.description: Both players were on the floor when Thomas pushed her fist into Clark's throat before getting up and stepping over her.

items.title: Turkey arrests more than 200 in crackdown before NATO summit
items.description: The Ankara prosecutor's office said 209 people were detained, while 32 others remained at large. Turkey has announced a ban on demonstrations amid preparations for next month's NATO summit in the capital.

items.title: Milei’s ‘promising’ inland provinces also hit by job crisis, company closures
items.description: While the government bets everything on trickledown results from mining and Vaca Muerta shale, private data reveals a generalised destruction of jobs and companies across Argentina’s inland provinces. Leer más

items.title: Messi eyes World Cup record and birthday win against Austria
items.description: Albiceleste captain celebrates his birthday on Wednesday and he will hope to hold the World Cup scoring record outright by then. Leer más

items.title: US Fed's preferred inflation gauge hits fresh three-year high
Skipper Lionel Messi on Austria win: ‘It was very even, very intense’
items.description: Argentina’s captain scores both goals in 2-0 victory over Austria; “We’ve already given the fans plenty to cheer about, but we’ll try to give them one more reason to celebrate," says skipper. Leer más

items.title: Toyota eyes lunar rover powered by regenerative fuel-cell tech
items.description: Toyota has teamed up with JAXA since 2019 to develop the manned lunar rover — which it dubbed the Lunar Cruiser — that they hope can be put on the moon in 2029.

items.title: Why is Germany taking a big stake in a major tankmaker?
items.description: The German government will buy a 40% stake in tankmaker KNDS. The deal is as much about control in the critical defense sector as it is about the Franco-German relationship.

items.title: Hundreds arrested in Nairobi on anniversary of 2024 Gen Z protests
items.description: More than 300 people were arrested during protests in Nairobi on Thursday, police say, as Kenya marked two years since a landmark Gen Z uprising.

items.title: Sir Keir Starmer resigns as UK PM, Andy Burnham favourite to take over
items.description: Andy Burnham, who secured a return to Parliament just last week, appeared poised to succeed Starmer as Labour leader and UK PM after would-be rival Wes Streeting offered him his support. Leer más

items.title: Messi rules the World Cup – but are Argentina too reliant on him?
items.description: Five goals in two games have confirmed the Argentine captain as the tournament's all-time leading scorer. But with Lautaro Martínez and Julián Álvarez yet to find the net, questions are growing about what happens if and when Messi's legs give out. Leer más

items.title: Police rule out terrorism, bomb attack in Lagos explosion
items.description: According to the police, investigators found no traces of explosive materials, detonators, initiation systems, explosive residues or blast patterns typically associated with explosive attacks. The post Police rule out terrorism, bomb attack in Lagos explosion appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Minimum wage has lost 40% of its purchasing power under Milei
items.description: Basic income for registered workers has plunged against November 2023 to levels even inferior to the 2001 crisis, according to a report by UBA Buenos Aires University’s Economics Faculty. Economic growth produces winners but they are not a majority. Leer más

items.title: World Cup 'Pride Match': FIFA refuses to ban rainbow flags despite Iran, Egypt objections
items.description: FIFA has rejected calls from Iran and Egypt to ban rainbow flags at their World Cup match in Seattle on Friday, despite both countries objecting to local Pride-themed celebrations linked to the fixture. FIFA says LGBTQ+ flags are permitted at all World Cup games under its Code of Conduct, despite different rules in Qatar's 2022 tournament. While FIFA has distanced itself from the city's events, it has made clear rainbow flags will be allowed for the games.

items.title: Tarique Rahman in China: Bangladesh discusses Teesta project in Beijing
items.description: China has said the Teesta River Comprehensive Management and Restoration Project would include dredging of more than 100 km of the river, which would allow better management of the river’s water and prevent erosion

items.title: Pakistan says Iran-U.S. talks expected to resume next week
items.description: In a statement issued late on June 24, 2026, Pakistan's foreign office spokesperson Tahir Andrabi said “parties are on the table” and that the process was going on

items.title: Ukraine, Poland smooth over WW2 dispute at Gdansk aid forum
items.description: Ukraine and its European allies are looking to encourage investment in Kyiv's post-war recovery. The first tranche of an EU loan has already arrived with more deals on the table. But relations with Poland are strained.

items.title: What Keir Starmer's resignation means for European security
items.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer was widely known for his support of Ukraine and his efforts towards a new EU–UK defense partnership. What will happen to those policies now that Starmer has announced his resignation?
